name: Donald Trump
summary: Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **TRUMP INSISTS THE DEAL IS ALIVE 'AT A RAPID PACE' WHILE IRAN SAYS IT WALKED — AND HE BROKERED A CONTESTED BEIRUT CAP THAT OPENED A RIFT WITH NETANYAHU. After Iran formally SUSPENDED the indirect talks on 1 June (over Israel's Lebanon campaign), Trump posted that talks are 'continuing, at a rapid pace, with the Islamic Republic of Iran' and that an MOU to reopen Hormuz 'could be reached as early as next week'; he dismissed the suspension ('started to get very boring,' doesn't care 'if talks are over') — the one-side-framing pattern, US side (deal = SUSPENDED Iran/CONFIRMED, 'continuing' US/CLAIMED). On Lebanon, Trump asked Netanyahu NOT to strike Beirut's southern suburbs and announced Israel and Hezbollah had agreed to 'halt hostilities'; Israel held off the Beirut strike but kept hitting south Lebanon, and Axios reported a 'tense phone conversation' between Trump and Netanyahu over the continued strikes — an open, public rift. The civil-military gap widened: Trump's definitive 'rapid pace' against CENTCOM's bounded 'measured and deliberate self-defence' language on the Goruk/Qeshm strikes and Iran's on-record suspension — the credible read is CENTCOM's, not the 'rapid pace' gloss. One think-tank take (House of Saud) reframed Iran's 1 June 'triple escalation' as aimed at the US Senate/Congressional opposition, not at Trump directly. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 2)**: Trump-specific deal triggers — encode the deal as SUSPENDED by Iran (CONFIRMED) / 'continuing' by Trump (CLAIMED); no signature, no 'deal alive' or 'deal dead' encoding. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30). **TRUMP ENDED THE 29 MAY SITUATION ROOM MEETING WITHOUT SIGNING — AND HIS PUBLIC ACCOUNT OF THE DEAL IS NOW DIRECTLY CONTRADICTED BY TEHRAN. Trump made no 'final determination' on the 60-day MOU (CONFIRMED non-signature — CNBC); Rubio blamed 'disagreements over a word, a sentence' and projected 'a few days.' Trump publicly insists Iran's ~1,000 lbs HEU 'will be destroyed' and that there will be 'no sanctions, no money, no nothing' (CLAIMED — US framing), which Fars rebutted point-blank (no destruction clause in the text; the centerpiece is a $12bn frozen-asset release). On the Accords, Trump escalated: he told the leaders of Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Jordan and Bahrain that joining the Abraham Accords is 'mandatory' after the Iran war — and per a US official the leaders, 'especially Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Pakistan, appeared stunned… there was silence on the line, and Trump joked and asked if they are still there' (REPORTED — Axios). Trump's HEU/'no money' claims and the 'mandatory' Accords demand both remain RHETORIC/CLAIMED — no signed deal, no normalization. NO trigger moves.** Day 90 of Hormuz blockade (Thursday May 28). **TRUMP HARDENED THE DEAL TERMS AND REFUSED SANCTIONS RELIEF EVEN AS AN MOU TO EXTEND THE CEASEFIRE 60 DAYS AWAITS HIS SIGNATURE. At the May 27 Cabinet meeting Trump said on the record (PBS, CNBC): 'No sanctions, no money, no nothing'; Iran's ~1,000 lbs of HEU 'will be destroyed' — in the US, in Iran, or 'at another acceptable location' — and he OPPOSES Russia OR China taking custody (Washington's shorthand: 'No dust, no dollars'). The White House dismissed an Iranian-state-TV-published draft framework (gradual Oman-mediated Hormuz reopening, US withdrawal, blockade lifting) as a 'COMPLETE FABRICATION' ('Nobody should believe what Iranian state media is putting out'). Reporting (US News, single US-official source) says an MOU to extend the ceasefire 60 days has been REACHED but Trump has NOT approved it. Trump is also under pressure from Iran hawks in his own party not to sign any deal that fails to address the nuclear program immediately.** Operationally, Trump's posture is the US-side mirror of Iran's 'graveyard' line — public maximalism while text is negotiated: he ordered/owns the second US 'defensive' strike (drones + ground-control station near Bandar Abbas) that drew Iran's first, intercepted retaliatory missile, yet both sides kept negotiating (NOT a return to war; Fact-Checker F1). The genuinely new negatives are the nuclear DEADLOCK (Araghchi) and the public sanctions-relief refusal, not the missile. **TRIGGER NOTE**: the 'publicly accepts/rejects the MOU OR pivots back to kinetic ultimatum (by 2026-05-31)' trigger STAYS watching — Trump did neither: he hardened TERMS (sanctions/HEU) while leaving the MOU unsigned, which is a third path (raise the price, don't walk away, don't sign). PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 of Hormuz blockade (Wednesday May 27; ~48h since the May 25 brief, no brief May 26). **THE MANDATORY-ABRAHAM-ACCORDS DEMAND WIDENED FROM SIX TO EIGHT NATIONS (UAE + BAHRAIN ADDED — both already signatories — so the demand is now 'all of these countries simultaneously') AND DREW PAKISTAN'S FLAT PUBLIC REJECTION: DefMin Khawaja Asif told Samaa TV joining 'clashes with our fundamental ideologies' (CONFIRMED). Trump (May 26 Truth Social) said the signatories 'should not be part of this Deal,' that refusal 'shows bad intention,' and told Saudi Arabia and Qatar to 'go first'; per a US official cited by Axios the leaders on the weekend call — 'especially those of Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Pakistan' — 'appeared stunned' (REPORTED, single Axios channel). Qatar has NOT publicly responded.** Trump then convened a rare **Camp David Cabinet meeting May 27** (attendees reported to include DNI Tulsi Gabbard): he vowed a favourable deal, said the regime's effort to 'outlast him won't work because I don't care about the midterms,' expressed confidence a deal is near, but cautioned the administration is 'not yet satisfied with the terms — and is willing to resume fighting if it can't secure its demands' (CONFIRMED RHETORIC). **The action behind those words is CONFIRMED: the US conducted self-defense strikes inside southern Iran on May 25 (struck IRGC mine-laying boats + a Bandar Abbas SAM site)** — leverage applied inside the negotiation, not a collapse of it. On the deal itself, Trump says reopening Hormuz is 'largely negotiated' (draft MOU: 60-day ceasefire extension, Hormuz reopened 'no tolls' + Iran clears mines, US lifts the port blockade + sanctions waivers, HEU 'disposal' mechanism, 'strong inspections') but US reporting names three unresolved blockers: Hormuz control, the nuclear file, and whether the ceasefire covers Israel's Hezbollah ops (Iran insists Lebanon is covered). **The Accords trigger added May 25 has FIRED on the rejection limb (Pakistan, CONFIRMED).** Net: simultaneous strikes + signatures are one coercive strategy; the convexity stays asymmetric to the downside. PRIOR (May 25): **TRUMP HELD THE 'NO RUSH' LINE AND BOLTED A MAXIMALIST NEW CONDITION ONTO THE IRAN DEAL — THE LARGEST SCOPE-EXPANSION OF THE CYCLE. IN MONDAY TRUTH SOCIAL POSTS HE WROTE 'NEGOTIATIONS WITH THE ISLAMIC REPUBLIC OF IRAN ARE PROCEEDING NICELY!' AND DECLARED IT 'SHOULD BE MANDATORY' THAT SIX MUSLIM NATIONS — SAUDI ARABIA, QATAR, PAKISTAN, EGYPT, JORDAN, TURKEY — 'SIMULTANEOUSLY, SIGN ONTO THE ABRAHAM ACCORDS,' STARTING WITH SAUDI AND QATAR, AND INVITED IRAN TO JOIN THE 'WORLD COALITION' IF IT SIGNS (CONFIRMED — NPR, WaPo, CNBC, ToI, AXIOS). PER AXIOS'S RAVID, TRUMP FLOATED THIS ON SATURDAY'S LEADERS' CALL; THE REACTION WAS 'SILENCE ON THE LINE.' THIS IS THE OPERATIVE EMERGENCE OF THE PREVIOUSLY-FLAGGED LINDSEY GRAHAM 'EXPANSIONIST' VECTOR — NOW TRUMP POLICY — AND IT VASTLY INCREASES THE DEAL'S DEGREES OF FREEDOM (RHETORIC / NEGOTIATING DEMAND — NO SIGNED ACCORDS, NO NAMED-COUNTRY ACCEPTANCE).** Simultaneously the deal is forming a US DOMESTIC-POLITICAL CONSTRAINT: SASC chair Roger Wicker (R-MS) warned that seeking a deal now risks 'a perception of weakness'; conservative hawks and Democrats alike voice 'bad deal' concerns; CNN framed the possible deal as 'almost as divisive as Trump's decision to wage war' (CONFIRMED — CNN Politics May 25). Wicker's hawkish-skeptic flank constrains how soft a deal Trump can sign, even as the Accords condition is partly an answer to that critique. (1) **Trump 'mandatory' Abraham Accords condition [NEW, RHETORIC / NEGOTIATING DEMAND]**: bolting Arab-Israeli normalisation onto a Hormuz/nuclear de-escalation deal hands every reluctant capital a reason to slow-walk; targeted Gulf/Muslim capitals met it with surprise and 'silence on the line' (Axios), and Gulf state media conspicuously did NOT amplify it. (2) **'No rush' hold continues**: the Sunday→Monday posture is 'proceeding nicely' + 'time is on our side' — neither acceptance nor rejection of the leaked MOU; the US blockade remains in force until any deal is 'certified and signed.' (3) **Cooling on imminence**: Rubio said talks are 'still a work in progress' with 'a pretty solid thing on the table' on Hormuz; both sides now converging on 'not imminent' (Iran FM Baghaei: 'no one can claim an imminent agreement is about to be signed'). (4) **Nuclear contradiction persists**: US admin sources (Axios) claim Iranians gave assurances 'verbal and in writing' that any permanent deal will include 'disposal' of all enriched uranium and that Mojtaba 'endorsed the broad template' — contradicted by Baghaei 'nuclear isn't part of discussions at this stage'; the Mojtaba HEU-retention directive is Day 4 of 14 with NO Iranian state-media confirmation/walk-back. **Structural reads**: the kinetic binary is now firmly subordinate to the deal-architecture binary; Monday added a maximalist scope-expansion (Abraham Accords) that raises the activation energy for any signature while the targeted capitals went silent — most likely an opening-bid overreach, but a potential poison pill that stalls the Iran deal. **MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW EXPIRED WITHOUT KINETIC ACTION; MAY 25 IS RHETORIC/DIPLOMATIC POSTURE — TRUMP NEITHER ACCEPTED NOR REJECTED THE MOU AND DID NOT PIVOT TO KINETIC.** PRIOR (May 24): **MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW EXPIRED WITHOUT KINETIC ACTION. TRUMP PIVOTED FROM 'I'LL LIKELY DECIDE TOMORROW' (SAT) TO 'TIME IS ON OUR SIDE… NOT TO RUSH' (SUN) — LARGEST SINGLE-DAY TRUMP-RHETORIC REVERSAL OF THE DEAL CYCLE. SATURDAY TRUTH SOCIAL POST CLAIMED 'AGREEMENT LARGELY NEGOTIATED' WITH PHONE CALLS TO 10 REGIONAL LEADERS (KSA/UAE/QATAR/PAKISTAN/TURKEY/EGYPT/JORDAN/BAHRAIN + NETANYAHU); SUNDAY POSTS RE-OPENED THE TIMELINE AND KEPT US BLOCKADE 'IN FULL FORCE AND EFFECT UNTIL AN AGREEMENT IS REACHED, CERTIFIED AND SIGNED.' WHITE HOUSE OFFICIALS PUBLICLY NAMED MOJTABA AS THE DECISION-LOCUS ('SEVERAL DAYS TO APPROVE'); SENIOR ADMIN OFFICIAL TOLD CBS IRAN 'IN PRINCIPLE AGREED' TO HEU DISPOSAL — CONTRADICTED BY IRAN FOREIGN MINISTRY AND IMPLICITLY BY MOJTABA DIRECTIVE.** (1) **Trump Saturday May 23 Truth Social (CONFIRMED — NPR primary, CBS, CNN, WaPo, CNBC)**: 'An Agreement has been largely negotiated, subject to finalization between the United States of America, the Islamic Republic of Iran, and the various other Countries,' with phone calls to KSA/UAE/Qatar/Pakistan/Turkey/Egypt/Jordan/Bahrain + Netanyahu about a 'Memorandum of Understanding pertaining to PEACE.' (2) **Trump Sunday May 24 (CONFIRMED — CBS News liveblog primary, CNBC, Inquirer)**: 'negotiations are proceeding in an orderly and constructive manner'; told negotiators 'not to rush into a deal' because 'time is on our side'; US blockade 'will remain in full force and effect until an agreement is reached, certified and signed.' The 24-hour gap between 'largely negotiated' and 'time is on our side' is the most operationally significant Trump-side rhetoric pivot of the deal cycle. (3) **CBS senior administration source (REPORTED — single anonymous channel)**: US and Iran have reached 'broad commitment on principles' and 'the Iranians have in principle agreed to a deal that would include the disposal of highly enriched uranium' — DIRECTLY CONTRADICTED by Iran FM Baghaei 'not yet accepted any actions on its nuclear programme' (Jerusalem Post). (4) **White House officials (REPORTED — JPost primary)**: 'did not expect a deal to be reached on Sunday'; Iranian Supreme Leader **Mojtaba Khamenei would take several days to approve any deal** — FIRST US-SIDE INSTITUTIONAL ACKNOWLEDGEMENT of Mojtaba as decision-locus. US 'still obstructing some clauses in the agreement, including the release of Iran's blocked assets.' (5) **Trump-Netanyahu private call (REPORTED — TOI sources)**: Trump assured Netanyahu he 'will not sign any final agreement without the conditions that Iran dismantle its entire nuclear program and remove all enriched uranium from the country' — directly contradicts US admin's public framing that Iran has 'in principle agreed' to disposal. Either Trump is double-tracking with Netanyahu vs the press, or admin source over-stated Iranian acceptance. (6) **Rubio in New Delhi for Quad bilateral with Jaishankar (CONFIRMED — US News, CNBC)**: 'progress had been made in the past 48 hours on an outline that could help resolve the situation around the Strait of Hormuz.' Quad meeting Tuesday May 26. (7) **Markets Friday close (CONFIRMED yfinance)**: Brent $103.54 (-5%+ on the week); WTI $96.60 (-8%+ on the week); VIX 16.70 at year lows; gold $4,523 (-3.9% MoM, -12.3% 3M underperformer). Cleanest paper-side deal-anticipation print of the cycle. (8) **Pakistan COAS Munir Tehran visit concluded Saturday May 23** with ISPR 'encouraging progress towards a final understanding' — not yet validated by matching Iranian state-media framing. **Qatari negotiating team in Tehran since Friday May 22** in coordination with US (REPORTED — Reuters via JPost) — dual-mediation architecture (Pakistan formal/military + Qatar substantive/IRGC backchannel) now operationally explicit with parallel delegations. **Structural reads**: (a) Trump's adoption of Iran's own 'time is on our side' framing within 24 hours validates Vali Nasr's 'time on Iran's side' thesis — both sides now slow; (b) the May 24 strike-window trigger added May 19 resolves UNTRIPPED_EXPIRED — no kinetic order, no strike directive, no Memorial Day weekend escalation through Sunday evening Tehran time; (c) the bifurcation thesis is now operating in real-time — Saturday 'largely negotiated' was Trump-side public anchoring, Sunday 'no rush' is the US side adjusting to the Trump-fast vs Iran-30-60-day asymmetric timeline mismatch; (d) the structural contradictions remain unresolved (Fars 'management' vs Trump 'opened'; admin 'in principle agreed to disposal' vs Baghaei 'not in current MOU'); markets are pricing deliberate-ambiguity resolution, Iranian regime architecture is preparing for deal-failure. **MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW DAY 5 OF 5 RESOLVES UNTRIPPED_EXPIRED — NO KINETIC ACTION.** PRIOR (May 23): TRUMP PUBLICLY ANCHORED THE DEAL DIRECTION ('EVERY DAY IT GETS BETTER AND BETTER'; 'GETTING A LOT CLOSER'), SOFTENED URANIUM LANGUAGE TO 'SATISFACTORILY HANDLED' (vs MAY 16 LETTER'S 'FULL DISMANTLEMENT'). SIMULTANEOUSLY '50/50' BETWEEN DEAL AND STRIKES; 'WILL LIKELY DECIDE TOMORROW'; CBS REPORTS US MILITARY/INTEL CANCELED MEMORIAL DAY PLANS. (1) **Trump Saturday May 23 framing (CBS primary, ToI/Haaretz liveblog primary)**: 'US and Iran getting a lot closer to finalizing a ceasefire deal'; 'every day it gets better and better'; would only accept deal where 'we get everything we want'; warned 'no country will ever be hit as hard' if no agreement; **softened uranium formulation: enriched uranium must be 'satisfactorily handled'** (vs May 16 letter's 'full dismantlement + zero enrichment within 2 months'). Times of Israel framed as '50/50' probability split between deal and strikes 'will likely decide tomorrow.' (2) **Rubio in New Delhi (Detroit News, Al Jazeera)**: 'some progress' made; 'a little bit of movement, and that's good'; 'there is a chance that, whether it's later today, tomorrow, in a couple days, we may have something to say.' (3) **CBS Pentagon-source channel (high-credibility) May 22-23**: US military and intelligence personnel canceled Memorial Day weekend plans (May 23-25 US) in anticipation of renewed strikes on Iran; Washington has 'yet to make a final decision.' Procedural pre-positioning consistent with Trump's May 18 'remain prepared for a full-scale assault at a moment's notice' framing. (4) **Pakistan's COAS Field Marshal Asim Munir visited Tehran May 22-23** at the same time as the strike-prep architecture (Al Jazeera primary, ARY News, ISPR primary, multi-source). Trump's verbal posture anchors deal direction; the mediation track is structurally escalated (Naqvi Interior-Minister → Munir COAS); the kinetic option is administratively pre-staged. (5) **Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan publicly praised Trump diplomacy + Pakistan mediation** + urged Iran to 'seize the opportunity to avoid escalation' (Al Arabiya May 22-23). **Structural reads**: (a) Trump's 'every day better' is incompatible with imminent strike orders — anchoring public posture in diplomatic direction; (b) 'satisfactorily handled' uranium formulation is meaningful softening from May 16 letter — opens verbal space for deal that does NOT require Mojtaba directive walk-back; (c) the '50/50 / will likely decide tomorrow' framing keeps May 24 strike-window trigger live but operationally moves binary into deal-acceptance lane. **The May 22 brief's bifurcation thesis is now operationally explicit in Trump's own language**: ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions track is what he is talking about ('every day better'); nuclear track is what he has quietly softened on ('satisfactorily handled' ≠ 'full dismantlement'). The May 22 brief's 'ceasefire-without-nuclear-concession' asymmetric path is now in Trump's own verbal posture. **Iranian-side counter-signal at maximum amplitude while Trump anchors deal direction**: Baghaei 'deep and significant disagreements remain'; Ghalibaf 'US not honest partner'; UN mission 'excessive demands'; Tasnim military source 'Iran preparing for resuming fighting.' Iran is signaling Munir visit ≠ breakthrough. **Memorial Day weekend timing is significant**: strike during May 23-25 would be politically maximally inflammatory in US domestic context AND structurally aligned with Trump administration's use of symbolic dates. Cancellation of personnel leave is operational read on kinetic option being live; rhetoric is political read. **Helima Croft 'Tuesday close vs $112' sustain-rule binary resolved cleanly as false positive**: Brent flat at $103-104 with deal-anticipation pricing dominant. PRIOR (May 22): **PAKISTAN-MEDIATED DRAFT DEAL TEXT LEAKED VIA AL ARABIYA MAY 21-22 — CEASEFIRE / HORMUZ FREEDOM-OF-NAVIGATION / SANCTIONS-PHASING ARCHITECTURE BUT NO URANIUM DISMANTLEMENT. TRUMP PUBLICLY PIVOTED TO 'IN NO HURRY' AND TOLD REPORTERS NETANYAHU 'WILL DO WHATEVER I WANT HIM TO DO.' GABBARD RESIGNED AS DNI MAY 22 (officially husband's bone cancer; reported Iran-policy split context). MOJTABA KHAMENEI'S FIRST POLICY DIRECTIVE LEAKED MAY 21 ORDERING ENRICHED URANIUM TO STAY INSIDE IRAN — directly forecloses Trump's May 16 letter nuclear basis. The May 22 '2-3 day Pakistan-mediation' window resolved AMBIGUOUSLY (partially_resolved_text_leaked_no_public_acceptance) — a text exists in leak architecture but no public US/Iran formal acceptance.** Across May 20-22: (a) Pakistan Interior Minister Naqvi met Iran FM Araghchi in Tehran multiple times (CONFIRMED — Washington Times, IRNA primary); (b) Al Arabiya late May 21 leaked what it called the 'final draft' with immediate comprehensive ceasefire + freedom-of-navigation guarantees through Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz + sanctions lifted in phases on Iranian compliance + talks on outstanding issues within 7 days — but NOT uranium dismantlement (REPORTED — Al Arabiya primary leak, ILNA/Tasnim confirm draft moving but trace back to same leak chain); (c) Trump told reporters May 20 he is 'in no hurry,' that Netanyahu 'will do whatever I want him to do,' and that he is 'open to a limited Iran deal that would only open the Strait of Hormuz and extend the ceasefire' (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — Times of Israel May 20 liveblog primary, CBS, CNN); (d) Trump-Netanyahu had a 'difficult' phone call May 20 with sources describing Netanyahu's 'hair was on fire' (CONFIRMED — Axios scoop primary, Jerusalem Post, CNN Politics 'Trump and Netanyahu diverge on Iran war's future'); (e) Rubio May 21 publicly said any deal would be 'unfeasible' if Iran continues pursuing a Strait of Hormuz tolling system (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — CBS News liveblog primary); (f) DNI Tulsi Gabbard resigned May 22, fourth Cabinet-level departure of the second term and second senior Iran-skeptic IC departure (after Joe Kent's March NCTC exit). **Market repricing**: Brent settled $104.52 May 22 (-5.3% cumulative across May 19-22 window), WTI broke $100 to $99.50, VIX 16.61 (cycle low), USD/INR retraced from intra-week record low ₹97.15 to ₹95.69 close on ~$5B in 48hr RBI intervention. **Croft 'Tuesday close vs $112' sustain-rule binary resolves to FALSE-POSITIVE SUSTAIN-RULE FIRE**: May 21-22 closes decisively below $108 mathematically falsified the May 18-19 2-session sustain rule. **Structural premium intact at $30+ above $73 baseline** even after tail-risk de-pricing $7-8. **Sadjadpour 'two hubris' frame at maximum operational expression**: civilian-government (Pezeshkian/Araghchi) procedurally engaged with Pakistan track; IRGC (May 21 'global offensive' threat — RHETORIC) and Khamenei's office (Mojtaba uranium directive — REPORTED) substantively NO. **Three Gulf editorial channels coordinated**: Al Arabiya (deal-text leak — Saudi positioning for deal credit); Al Jazeera (May 21 'Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire' — Qatar framing Israel as deal-spoiler); The National (UAE restraint). **The kinetic axis is no longer the binding constraint; the deal-architecture axis is, and it has BIFURCATED**: a ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions deal looks executable on the operational track but the nuclear deliverable looks foreclosed by Mojtaba's directive. **Asymmetric path**: ceasefire deliverable in days; nuclear deliverable foreclosed. PRIOR (May 19): **TRUMP POSTPONED THE PLANNED MAY 19 STRIKE LATE MAY 18 US TIME — TRUTH SOCIAL: 'WE WILL NOT BE DOING THE SCHEDULED ATTACK OF IRAN TOMORROW' AT THE REQUEST OF QATAR (EMIR TAMIM), SAUDI ARABIA (MBS), AND UAE (MBZ) FOR A '2-3 DAY / FIVE-DAY' WINDOW IN WHICH 'SERIOUS NEGOTIATIONS' ARE RUNNING THROUGH PAKISTAN.** The post explicitly framed the postponement as a pause, not a cancellation: Trump said he had instructed the US military to 'remain prepared for a full-scale assault on Iran at a moment's notice if negotiations fail to produce an acceptable agreement' (CONFIRMED ACTION — Trump Truth Social primary + CNBC + Bloomberg + NPR + Al Arabiya + Israel Hayom + Pravda Trump translation). At the White House Monday evening, Trump told reporters Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE 'and some others' asked him to put the strike off for 'two or three days, a short period of time, because they think they are getting very close to making a deal.' **The NYT 'as soon as next week' trigger (added May 17) resolves to POSTPONED_WITHIN_WINDOW rather than untripped_expired** — Trump's own framing keeps the kinetic option active inside the May 24 window. Iran state TV (IRIB) called the postponement a 'retreat' based on 'fear' via broadcast ticker and X account — converting the postponement into an Iranian rhetorical win, deepening the trust deficit on the negotiating track. **Market repricing**: Brent fell -2% to ~$109.15 on the post (CNBC primary, NDTV Profit), partly inverting Monday's $111.47 gap; Brent settled $110.32 (-1.6% 1D); WTI -4.7% to $103.57; VIX -3.3% to 17.82; RBOB -4.8%; Heating Oil -3.2%. **The structural premium is intact**: at $110.32, Brent is still 51% above the $73 pre-crisis baseline; Hormuz remains effectively closed (~1-30 vessels/day vs 138 pre-war); AWRP 4-6x pre-crisis; structural-bid trio (XLE +1.9%, Frontline +2.9%, Cheniere +2.4%, Lockheed +2.4% on $879M F-35 contract) all UP on a Brent-down day — premium has migrated out of crude flat-price into ton-miles, LNG basis, and defense backlog. **Helima Croft 'Tuesday close vs $112' binary resolves toward FALSE-POSITIVE SUSTAIN-RULE FIRE pending close**: StanChart $130-145 path loses momentum; Goldman base case ($120 Q3 / $115 Q4 if Hormuz remains mostly shut) re-anchors as central. **Tuesday May 19 NSC binary resolved in the diplomatic direction with hardening overlays**: (a) kinetic axis slipped 48-120 hours; (b) diplomatic axis hardened (Iran IRIB 'retreat-based-on-fear' framing); (c) proxy axis re-armed rhetorically (Houthis announced 'maritime blockade' of Haifa port — CONFIRMED RHETORIC, NO operational follow-through); (d) India fuel pass-through fired (2nd hike in a week, ~90 paise petrol/diesel — April 27 trigger ACTIVE_FIRED inside its 30-day window). Iran FM spokesman Baghaei (via Tasnim) confirmed counter-proposal delivered through Pakistan with demands: unfreezing of frozen assets, lifting of sanctions, end of US blockade of Iranian ports, 'stoppage of fighting in all aspects' including Lebanon, and compensation for war damages — far apart from US zero-enrichment red line. **Sadjadpour 'two hubris' frame operationally validated in real-time**: Trump's performative-dealmaker brand permitted accepting the Gulf-mediated postponement because it credits him; Iran's institutional architecture continues to require public framing of any pause as adversary-weakness. **The cleanest read: postponement, not de-escalation. The kinetic option remains administratively pre-staged.** PRIOR (May 18 — Monday): **TUESDAY MAY 19 NSC MEETING IS THE OPERATIONAL BINARY — Day 1 of 6 remaining in NYT 'as soon as next week' window (closes May 24).** Per Haaretz May 18 'As Trump Hesitates, Israel Prepares for Renewed Iran Strikes' (REPORTED — single Israeli sourcing chain), Trump is expected to convene his top national security team Tuesday May 19 to discuss options for military action. **No White House public confirmation through Monday morning IST.** **CONFIRMED WEEKEND EVENTS**: (i) Trump-Netanyahu phone call Sunday May 17 ahead of limited Israeli security cabinet meeting, both sides framing as discussion of 'renewing the war with Iran' (CONFIRMED ACTION); Netanyahu addressed Cabinet: 'our eyes are also open' regarding Iran, 'prepared for any scenario'; (ii) Trump posted **'for Iran, the clock is ticking'** on Truth Social later Sunday (CONFIRMED RHETORIC). Haaretz May 18 frames the operational binary: Trump described as hesitating despite NYT preparation reporting and his own escalatory rhetoric, while Israeli defense establishment positioning for unilateral preparation. **The Haaretz frame — 'As Trump Hesitates, Israel Prepares' — is the cleanest publicly stated US-Israel divergence signal of the cycle**: at minimum the senior IDF reading; at maximum a deliberate Israeli leak intended to pressure Trump toward action. **PRIOR (May 17): TRUMP PIVOTS FROM RHETORICAL REVERSAL TO KINETIC PREPARATION POSTURE.** On Sunday morning IST Trump posted on Truth Social an AI-generated graphic of himself + US Navy admiral with stormy waters and an Iranian-flagged ship, captioned **'It Was The Calm Before the Storm'** (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — Times of Israel May 17 liveblog primary + Ynet + Israel National News + India TV + Egypt Today + IANS + Arab Times + Al Bawaba); a second post showed an animation of a US warship targeting an aircraft marked with the Iranian flag, with Trump's voice ordering 'Okay, we have it in our sight. Fire!' (CONFIRMED RHETORIC). **The post-summit reversal pattern has now hardened from rhetoric into REPORTED military preparation** — NYT (Friday May 15 / Saturday May 16, citing two Middle Eastern officials) reported Israel + US are carrying out 'most intense preparations yet to renew attacks on Iran possibly as early as next week,' with options including (i) **commando operation to extract nuclear material believed buried under rubble at struck nuclear sites** ('thousands of supporting forces to create a perimeter,' US officials acknowledged 'would risk several casualties' and likely engage Iranian ground troops); and (ii) **ground forces to seize Kharg Island** (handles up to 90% of Iranian oil exports). NYT scoop amplified by Times of Israel May 16 liveblog, Jerusalem Post 'US-Israel Iran attacks could resume next week' article 896353, Gulf News 'Operation Epic Fury 2.0,' Fox News 'Next US move on Iran: Seize Kharg Island,' CNN Politics 'Trump returns from China with no Iran breakthrough — and a decision to make' (framed as decision between 'more strikes' vs 'continued focus on diplomacy,' Pentagon hawks favouring strikes / Vance-diplomacy track on other side). **NO on-record Pentagon/White House confirmation of operation planning.** **PARALLEL DIPLOMATIC TRACK INTENSIFIES (CONFIRMED ACTION):** Pakistani Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi arrived Tehran Saturday May 16 on 2-day official visit explicitly described in Pakistani official communications as 'to facilitate' US-Iran peace talks. **Trump can simultaneously prepare strikes and hold an open mediation channel — diplomatic and kinetic tracks now running in clear tension, not sequence.** This is structurally the SIXTH high-profile public posture shift in 30 days (joint statement Thursday → 'decimation' Truth Social same day → new letter Saturday → 'Calm Before the Storm' Sunday + parallel Pakistan-mediation track) — the rhetorical-volatility pattern is now structurally embedded as base case, not tail. **Vali Nasr structural-mismatch thesis at sharpest of cycle:** Trump's 'Calm Before the Storm' post + NYT preparation reporting compresses time horizon to days/weeks; Iran's six-pillar architecture (Araghchi/Shamkhani/Pezeshkian/Akraminia/IRGC/now Naqvi-mediation) is designed for a longer horizon. The structural mismatch will resolve one of two ways: Trump backs off the 2-month timeline OR kinetic action commences. **Helima Croft framing on test point of cycle:** Brent Monday close above $108 = 2-session-sustain rule met = StanChart $130-145 path activates. (2) **Camp David Cabinet meeting May 27 [CONFIRMED RHETORIC]**: Trump convened a rare Camp David Cabinet meeting during the deal endgame (attendees reported to include DNI Tulsi Gabbard). He vowed a favourable deal, said the regime's effort to 'outlast him won't work because I don't care about the midterms,' expressed confidence a deal is near, but cautioned the administration is 'not yet satisfied with the terms — and is willing to resume fighting if it can't secure its demands.' recent_signals.headline: **TRUMP HELD 'NO RUSH' AND ATTACHED A MAXIMALIST NEW CONDITION — 'MANDATORY' ABRAHAM ACCORDS SIGNATURES BY SIX MUSLIM NATIONS AS PART OF THE IRAN DEAL, THE LARGEST SCOPE-EXPANSION OF THE CYCLE. MONDAY POSTS: 'NEGOTIATIONS… ARE PROCEEDING NICELY!' + IT 'SHOULD BE MANDATORY' THAT SAUDI ARABIA, QATAR, PAKISTAN, EGYPT, JORDAN, TURKEY 'SIMULTANEOUSLY, SIGN ONTO THE ABRAHAM ACCORDS,' STARTING WITH SAUDI AND QATAR; IRAN INVITED INTO THE 'WORLD COALITION' IF IT SIGNS. PER AXIOS, THE LEADERS MET IT WITH 'SILENCE ON THE LINE.' US DOMESTIC SKEPTICISM FORMING ON BOTH FLANKS — WICKER (R, SASC CHAIR): SEEKING A DEAL NOW RISKS 'A PERCEPTION OF WEAKNESS.'** (1) **Trump Abraham Accords 'mandatory' demand (CONFIRMED Trump posts + multi-outlet — NPR May 25, Washington Post May 25, CNBC May 25, Times of Israel May 25, Axios May 24; RHETORIC / NEGOTIATING DEMAND, no signed accords)**: in Monday-morning Truth Social posts Trump wrote 'Negotiations with the Islamic Republic of Iran are proceeding nicely!' and separately declared it 'should be mandatory that all of these Countries, at a minimum, simultaneously, sign onto the Abraham Accords,' naming Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Pakistan, Egypt, Jordan and Turkey, saying the expansion 'should start with the immediate signing by Saudi Arabia and Qatar, and everybody else should follow suit,' and invited Iran itself to join the 'World Coalition' if it signs. Per Axios's Barak Ravid, Trump first floated this on Saturday's group call with the eight-plus leaders; those 'especially of Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Pakistan, who don't have formal diplomatic relations with Israel, were surprised… there was silence on the line, and Trump joked and asked if they are still there' (REPORTED — Axios single-sourcing on the reaction). UAE's MbZ — already an Accords signatory — said he supports it. **This is the operative emergence of the Lindsey Graham 'expansionist' vector the May 24 record flagged as not-yet-operative: it is now Trump policy.** The intelligence read: bolting Arab-Israeli normalisation onto a Hormuz/nuclear de-escalation deal vastly increases the deal's degrees of freedom and hands every reluctant capital a reason to slow-walk; the absence of enthusiastic Gulf state-media uptake is itself the intelligence that the demand is unwelcome. (2) **US domestic skepticism forming on both flanks (CONFIRMED — CNN Politics May 25, CBS/NPR corroboration)**: SASC chair Roger Wicker (R-MS) warned that seeking a deal now risks 'a perception of weakness'; conservative hawks and Democrats alike are voicing 'bad deal' concerns; CNN framed the possible deal as 'almost as divisive as Trump's decision to wage war.' This extends the Graham(expansionist)/Van Hollen(restrictionist) framing with a hawkish-skeptic sub-current (Wicker) worried the deal is too soft — a genuine domestic-political constraint on how soft a deal Trump can sign, and partly the reason Trump bolted on the maximalist Accords condition. (3) **'No rush' hold + cooling on imminence**: Trump's 'proceeding nicely' framing sustains the Sunday 'time is on our side / not to rush' posture — he neither accepted nor rejected the leaked MOU and did not pivot to a kinetic ultimatum. Rubio matched the cooling (CNN liveblog May 25): negotiations 'still a work in progress,' with 'a pretty solid thing on the table' on Hormuz; Iran FM Baghaei: 'no one can claim that… an imminent agreement is about to be signed.' (4) **Doha venue + nuclear contradiction (context)**: a senior Iranian delegation (Ghalibaf, Araghchi, central-bank governor Hemmati) arrived in Doha to work Hormuz + HEU + frozen-asset unfreezing with Qatar's PM (CONFIRMED ACTION). US admin sources (Axios) say the Iranians gave 'verbal and in writing' assurances any permanent deal will include 'disposal' of all enriched uranium and that Mojtaba 'endorsed the broad template' — contradicted by Baghaei 'nuclear isn't part of discussions at this stage'; the Mojtaba HEU-retention directive is Day 4 of 14 with NO Iranian state-media confirmation/walk-back. **Structural reads**: (a) the Abraham Accords demand is most likely an opening-bid overreach, but it raises the activation energy for any signature and is the single largest scope-expansion of the cycle; (b) the kinetic binary is now firmly subordinate to the deal-architecture binary — Monday's posture was 'no rush' + scope-expansion, neither accept nor reject; (c) Wicker's 'perception of weakness' flank is a structural constraint that, combined with the Accords poison-pill risk and the unresolved Hormuz-management/HEU contradictions, keeps the deal's convexity asymmetric to the downside. **MAY 25 IS RHETORIC / DIPLOMATIC POSTURE — NOT CONFIRMED KINETIC ACTION.**

recent_signals.headline: **TRUMP MAY 23 ANCHORED THE DEAL DIRECTION: 'EVERY DAY IT GETS BETTER AND BETTER'; 'GETTING A LOT CLOSER'; URANIUM MUST BE 'SATISFACTORILY HANDLED' (vs MAY 16 LETTER 'FULL DISMANTLEMENT'). SIMULTANEOUSLY '50/50' + 'WILL LIKELY DECIDE TOMORROW' + CBS REPORTS US MILITARY/INTEL CANCELED MEMORIAL DAY WEEKEND PLANS. MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW DAY 5 OF 5.** (1) **Trump May 23 public framing** (CBS News liveblog primary, Times of Israel May 23 liveblog primary, Haaretz May 23 liveblog primary): 'getting a lot closer' to finalizing ceasefire deal with Iran; 'every day it gets better and better'; would only accept deal where 'we get everything we want'; warned 'no country will ever be hit as hard' if no agreement. Two non-negotiables: Iran cannot obtain nuclear weapons; enriched uranium must be **'satisfactorily handled'** — meaningful softening from May 16 letter's 'full dismantling + zero enrichment within 2 months.' Times of Israel framed as '50/50' probability split between deal and strikes 'will likely decide tomorrow.' (2) **Rubio in New Delhi** (Al Jazeera May 22-23 primary, Detroit News May 22 wire): 'some progress' made; 'a little bit of movement, and that's good'; 'there is a chance that, whether it's later today, tomorrow, in a couple days, we may have something to say.' (3) **CBS Pentagon-source channel May 22-23**: US military and intelligence personnel canceled Memorial Day weekend plans (May 23-25 US) in anticipation of renewed strikes on Iran; Washington has 'yet to make a final decision.' Procedural pre-positioning, administrative readiness independent of order. (4) **Pakistan COAS Field Marshal Asim Munir conducted ~24-hour Tehran visit May 22-23** — mediation track structurally escalated from Interior Minister Naqvi to military-chief level (Al Jazeera primary, ARY News, ISPR primary, Geo TV, Daily Pakistan, Times of Israel). Met Pezeshkian + Ghalibaf + Araghchi + Momeni. ISPR: 'short but highly productive' with 'encouraging progress towards a final understanding.' (6) **Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan publicly praised Trump diplomacy + Pakistan mediation** + urged Iran to 'seize the opportunity to avoid escalation' (Al Arabiya May 22-23 primary) — Gulf editorial coordination intact, Saudi positioning for primary deal credit. **Structural reads**: (a) Trump's 'every day better' is incompatible with imminent strike orders — anchoring public posture in diplomatic direction; (b) 'satisfactorily handled' uranium formulation opens verbal space for deal that does NOT require Mojtaba directive walk-back — US verbally accommodating directive's substance even without formal deal; (c) the '50/50 / will likely decide tomorrow' framing keeps the May 24 strike-window trigger live but operationally moves the binary into the deal-acceptance lane. **The May 22 brief's bifurcation thesis is now operationally explicit in Trump's own language**: ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions track is what he is talking about ('every day better'); nuclear track is what he has quietly softened on ('satisfactorily handled' ≠ 'full dismantlement'). **Memorial Day weekend timing**: strike during May 23-25 would be politically maximally inflammatory in US domestic context AND structurally aligned with Trump administration use of symbolic dates. Cancellation of personnel leave is operational read on kinetic option being live; rhetoric is political read. **The Iranian-US cross-side timeline gap** (Trump 'could be a couple days' vs Baghaei '30-60 days') is the structural mismatch — Trump's brand requires fast deliverables; Iran's institutional architecture requires longer horizons. **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated** by Trump's softer uranium formulation — Iran did not have to publicly concede the nuclear track and US president has verbally accommodated.

recent_signals.headline: **MAY 22 PAKISTAN-MEDIATION WINDOW CLOSES WITH LEAKED DRAFT TEXT BUT NO PUBLIC ACCEPTANCE. TRUMP PIVOTS PUBLIC FRAMING TO 'IN NO HURRY' AND 'NETANYAHU WILL DO WHATEVER I WANT HIM TO DO.' GABBARD RESIGNS AS DNI. MOJTABA'S FIRST POLICY DIRECTIVE LEAKED — URANIUM STAYS IN IRAN, FORECLOSES NUCLEAR TRACK.** Across May 20-22 the cycle shifted from kinetic-binary to deal-architecture-bifurcation. (2) **Trump May 20 White House (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — Times of Israel May 20 liveblog primary, CBS News, CNN Politics, The Week)**: 'in no hurry to reach an agreement,' Netanyahu 'will do whatever I want him to do,' 'open to a limited Iran deal that would only open the Strait of Hormuz and extend the ceasefire,' and on May 18 was 'an hour away' from ordering new strikes before Gulf allies asked him to wait. **The 2-3 day Pakistan-mediation window now converts into open-ended 'no hurry' framing.** (3) **Axios scoop May 20**: Trump-Netanyahu 'difficult' call with sources describing Netanyahu's 'hair was on fire' after — wanted to continue strikes as planned. (6) **DNI Gabbard resigned May 22 (CONFIRMED — PBS NewsHour, WHSV, NewsNation, MSNBC primary)** citing husband's bone cancer; msnow reported 'rumblings' of Iran-policy split.
